briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby North Road BIA Renewal for Five Years Starting April 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would renew the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pool resources to improve the area — for five years from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031. The proposed bylaw would grant the Burnaby North Road Business Association up to $1,755,147.81 over that period for street improvements, graffiti removal, and business promotion, funded through a special property tax on commercial and light-industrial property owners within the BIA boundary.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property falls within the Burnaby North Road BIA boundary — you may owe a new local service tax.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw — proposed start date takes effect
Burnaby North Road BIA — annual budget submission deadline to City
Burnaby North Road BIA — annual audited financial statement deadline
Burnaby North Road BIA — proposed five-year term ends

briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Receive Update: Developer Forfeits $1.36M Instead of Building Fraser River Pier
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive an information report on April 14, 2026, about a developer's decision to forfeit $1,363,500 in security rather than build a planned public pier on the Fraser River at Riverbend Business Park. The developer, Oxford Properties, could not get the required provincial land approval to build on the river, so under a legal agreement (called a Section 219 Covenant — a registered condition tied to the property title), they are allowed to pay out instead. The City will hold the funds and decide later how to spend them on public amenities.
briefs.action_required: Watch for future Council decisions on how the $1.36M will be spent on public amenities near the Fraser Foreshore.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Amenity Pier Covenant report received for information

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Update on Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth
briefs.summary: Council will consider an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience, a group of business and community leaders working to identify ways to strengthen Burnaby's local economy. The Task Force is still mid-process — it has held five workshops so far and is set to deliver final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026 — they may affect local business support programs.

key_dates.label: Mayor's Task Force final recommendations presented to Burnaby Council

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider Rezoning 5035 Beresford Street for 20-Storey Rental Tower
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on advancing a rezoning application that would allow a 20-storey rental apartment building with 215 units to be built at 5035 Beresford Street in Burnaby's Royal Oak neighbourhood. The site currently holds a printing shop, and the proposed rezoning would change its land-use classification — meaning the rules about what can be built there — from industrial to a residential development zone.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meeting agendas for the First and Second Reading vote date on this rezoning bylaw.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-11 rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading council meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on hiring an outside expert to review Burnaby's financial practices
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on bringing in an independent (outside, unbiased) reviewer to look closely at how the City plans its budget, manages day-to-day spending, and decides which big projects to fund. The review is meant to help protect the City's financial health and reduce the risk of steep tax increases or rising service costs for businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — it could signal future changes to business taxes or city service costs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — external financial review approval

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Professional Services, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.67M fire protection services contract renewal — Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with A.W. Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. to inspect, maintain, and repair fire protection and emergency lighting systems at City-owned buildings. The proposed three-year renewal would run from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029, at an estimated cost of $1,666,061 including taxes.
briefs.action_required: If you supply fire protection services, watch this contract — it signals Burnaby's vendor and pricing expectations.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fire protection services contract renewal start date
Burnaby fire protection services contract renewal end date

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Vote on $394,942 Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Renewal
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on renewing a three-year software license subscription with Long View Systems Corporation for the City's Cisco WebEx Contact Center — the system that handles phone calls to Burnaby's Customer Service Centre. The proposed contract would run from May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029, at an estimated cost of $394,942 including taxes.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — monitor the April 14, 2026 council decision if you work with City IT services.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote on Cisco WebEx Contact Center contract renewal
Cisco WebEx Contact Center renewal contract start date — if approved

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract change for Burnaby city building HVAC services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a contract change worth $579,600 (including taxes) with Just Mechanical Ltd. to keep heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems running in city-owned buildings. This is a short-term extension to avoid any gap in service while the City runs a new competitive bidding process to find a long-term provider.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14, 2026 council meeting outcome if you provide HVAC or facilities services — a new RFP is coming.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — HVAC contract change vote
Just Mechanical interim HVAC contract end date — Burnaby

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract extension for Rogers cellular services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change that would pay Rogers Communications Canada Inc. up to $450,000 (including taxes) to keep the city's cellular phone and data services running for roughly nine more months. This is a temporary, month-to-month extension while the city wraps up negotiations with a new provider selected through a public bidding process.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — monitor the April 14, 2026 council decision if you supply telecom services to the city.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M contract for Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving work
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. to replace 16 culverts (underground drainage pipes) and repave the Central Valley Greenway trail between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road. The work is already budgeted in the City's 2026–2030 Financial Plan, with funding split between City reserves and TransLink.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near the Central Valley Greenway trail.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Central Valley Greenway culvert contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M contract for Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. to replace an undersized culvert at Jerry Rogers Creek with a larger box culvert built to handle major flood events. The project also includes replacing nearby water and sewer pipes. Council will consider this at its April 14, 2026 meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — construction near Jerry Rogers Creek at Marine Drive may affect nearby businesses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Jerry Rogers Creek culvert contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M contract for modular four-plex housing pilot in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular (factory-built) four-unit residential building at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The three-storey building would be constructed off-site and assembled on location, with the City retaining ownership and renting out the units — testing whether this building method is a cost-effective way to add housing in lower-density neighbourhoods.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14, 2026 council meeting decision — this pilot could shape future modular housing development rules in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Granting FortisBC a right-of-way for natural gas works on Burnaby Mountain
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on granting FortisBC Energy Inc. a Statutory Right-of-Way (SRW) — a legal permission to access and use a strip of city-owned land — over portions of 7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way. If approved, this would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure needed to service the Mintara restaurant and other facilities in the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the April 14, 2026 Council meeting outcome if your business operates near Burnaby Mountain or relies on FortisBC gas service.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — FortisBC Burnaby Mountain SRW approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Rejoining Building Benchmark BC Energy Tracking Program for Year 7
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on whether to continue the city's participation in Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) for Year 7 (2026). If approved, the city would keep tracking and sharing energy use and emissions data for 30 civic buildings, and promote the free program to private building owners in Burnaby — all within the existing $16,500 budget already set aside for this purpose.
briefs.action_required: If you own a commercial or multi-family building in Burnaby, watch for the city's BBBC recruitment campaign to sign up for free energy tracking.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Building Benchmark BC Year 7 participation approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on submitting Burnaby's first provincial housing target progress report
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on authorizing staff to submit Burnaby's first Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. The province has ordered Burnaby to build 10,240 net new homes by August 2030, and this report covers the first six months of that period (Sept 1, 2025 – Feb 28, 2026), during which approximately 910 net new units were completed. While Burnaby is currently on track for Year 1, slowing construction starts and a pause in provincial affordable housing funding are raising concerns about meeting future targets.
briefs.action_required: If you develop or own rental property in Burnaby, monitor council's housing target decisions — they shape zoning and approval rules.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Target Progress Report — Year 1b submission deadline to province
Burnaby Development Permit system (Form & Character, Streamside, Tenant Protection) takes effect

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on bylaw changes for e-bike share and e-scooter programs in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on amendments to three city bylaws that would set the rules for a new shared e-bike rental program and bring city rules in line with BC's provincial e-scooter pilot (which allows privately owned electric kick scooters on Burnaby streets). The proposed changes would define where these devices can ride and park, create designated parking zones for shared e-bikes, and set fines for riding in areas not approved for these devices.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new riding and parking rules for e-bikes and e-scooters could affect your business operations.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — e-bike share and e-scooter bylaw amendments vote

briefs.title: Council to consider approving Burnaby Housing Authority's 2025 financials and 2026 budget
briefs.summary: At its April 14, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council is set to receive the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) first-ever audited financial statements and its 2026 operating budget. Receiving these documents would unlock $2 million in City operating funding for the BHA — a City-owned corporation building affordable and market rental housing at two Burnaby sites.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it unlocks $2M in housing funding that could affect local development activity.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — BHA financials and 2026 budget up for approval
BHA $25M investment certificate matures — funding availability date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby Housing Authority annual governance and board appointments for 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on routine annual housekeeping matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) — a city-owned housing company. If approved, Council would skip a formal annual general meeting (AGM), appoint KPMG as auditor, confirm December 12, 2026 as the official reference date, accept the BHA's 2025 financial statements, and formally appoint 10 directors to the BHA board for two- or three-year terms.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if you follow Burnaby's affordable housing development activity.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Authority annual reference date — BHA AGM waiver takes effect

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to consider: North Road BIA renewal blocked by sufficient owner opposition
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report showing that enough property owners along North Road voted against renewing the Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where businesses pay an extra tax to fund local improvements. Because more than 50% of property owners (by both number of properties and total property value) submitted formal opposition, the proposed renewal bylaw cannot move forward.
briefs.action_required: If you own or operate a business in the North Road BIA, watch for updates on a possible re-application in fall 2026.

key_dates.label: Earliest possible North Road BIA renewal re-attempt — fall 2026 window opens

briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Vote on New Chief Election Officer for Burnaby's 2026 Municipal Election
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the October 17, 2026 General Local Election. The previous officer is on leave and not expected to return in time, so Council will consider replacing her with Back, an experienced administrator who has worked for the City of Burnaby before.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects who will run Burnaby's 2026 municipal election.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 General Local Election day

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Declaring April 28th the Official Day of Mourning for Workers
briefs.summary: At its April 14, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council will consider a request from the New Westminster & District Labour Council to officially declare April 28th as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job.' Council will also be asked to observe a minute of silence in chambers and encourage city staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28th.
briefs.action_required: Mark April 28th on your calendar — consider observing a minute of silence with your staff at 11:00 a.m.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Day of Mourning delegation presentation
Day of Mourning for Workers — proposed Burnaby observance date

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Construction & Development, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Letter Urging Federal Action on Chlorine Rail Transport Safety
briefs.summary: At a Special Council meeting on April 1, 2026, Burnaby Council voted unanimously to have Mayor Hurley write a letter to the federal Minister of Transport raising serious safety concerns about liquid chlorine being transported by rail through densely populated areas of the Lower Mainland. The concern stems from a nearby chlorine plant in North Vancouver that ships an average of four rail cars of liquid chlorine through Burnaby every day — a risk that presenters say has not been fully assessed in a pending North Vancouver bylaw amendment decision.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near a Burnaby rail corridor, monitor this issue — a chlorine release could trigger emergency evacuations.

key_dates.label: North Vancouver chlorine bylaw amendment — third reading scheduled

briefs.title: Burnaby Council March 24 Meeting: Key Votes on Housing, Zoning, and City Contracts
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several items affecting local business and development. Council approved a $4.2M water metering contract, renewed an asphalt paving contract, and advanced multiple rezoning applications — including a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway and a 5-storey self-storage facility at 2757 Norland Avenue — to the next stage of the approval process. These rezoning bylaws (rules that change how land can be used) still require further readings and final adoption before they take effect.
briefs.action_required: If you own property or a business near any of the rezoning sites, monitor upcoming Council meetings for public hearing dates.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics
